expectations.text: DashboardLayout이 folder 기반 index.tsx 패턴으로 정리된다
expectations.evidence: Output contains components/dashboard/dashboardLayout/index.tsx with DashboardLayout as default export, composing Sidebar, Header, and MobileNav sub-components. Matches the ProductCard/index.tsx folder-based pattern described in the transcript.

No flat DashboardLayout.tsx file exists in the outputs directory. Only the folder-based structure at components/dashboard/dashboardLayout/ is present. The transcript does not mention retaining the original flat file.

Sidebar.tsx contains an <aside> element with data-testid="dashboard-sidebar", rendering logo, nav links, and a settings footer. It is composed into the main layout via <Sidebar currentPath={currentPath} /> in index.tsx.

Header.tsx contains a <header> element with data-testid="dashboard-header", rendering a mobile logo, desktop title, and right slot with notification bell and avatar. Composed via <Header title={headerTitle} right={headerRight} /> in index.tsx.
index.tsx contains <main className="flex-1 overflow-y-auto px-4 py-6 pb-20 md:px-6 md:pb-6 lg:px-8" data-testid="dashboard-main">{children}</main> rendering children content.
DashboardLayoutProps includes 'children: ReactNode' as a required prop. The component renders {children} inside the <main> tag, acting purely as a shell/layout wrapper.

Responsive classes present throughout: Sidebar uses 'hidden md:flex md:w-60 lg:w-64'; Header uses 'md:px-6' and 'md:hidden'/'md:block' for mobile/desktop switching; MobileNav uses 'md:hidden'; main uses 'px-4 md:px-6 lg:px-8 pb-20 md:pb-6'.

expectations.text: 사용처 import가 새 경로에 맞게 정리된다
expectations.evidence: The transcript notes that app/dashboard/page.tsx 'does not import DashboardLayout', so there was no existing import to update. No output file shows an updated import path. While this may mean there was nothing to fix, there is no positive evidence of import path updates in the outputs.

expectations.text: useEffect, fetch, axios, useQuery, useMutation 호출이 없다
expectations.evidence: Grep search for 'useEffect|fetch(|axios|useQuery|useMutation' across all four output files returned zero matches. The components are purely visual with no side effects or data fetching.
The folder path is components/dashboard/dashboardLayout/ — 'dashboardLayout' is camelCase as required.
All four output files are located under components/dashboard/dashboardLayout/, which is under components/dashboard/ as required.

claims.claim: All data-testid attributes preserved from original component
claims.evidence: Output files contain data-testid attributes: dashboard-sidebar, sidebar-logo, sidebar-nav-*, dashboard-header, header-logo-mobile, dashboard-header-title, dashboard-header-right, dashboard-mobile-nav, mobile-nav-*, dashboard-main

claims.claim: Named Props export + default function export pattern matches ProductCard/index.tsx
claims.evidence: All four files export a named Props interface (DashboardLayoutProps, HeaderProps, SidebarProps, MobileNavProps) and a default function export, matching the described convention

claims.claim: Zero errors in new files from tsc and eslint
claims.evidence: Claimed in transcript steps 7-8 but cannot independently verify from outputs alone — no lint/tsc output files provided
All files contain dark: prefixed Tailwind classes (dark:bg-zinc-950, dark:border-zinc-800, dark:bg-zinc-900, dark:text-zinc-50, etc.)

suggestions.assertion: 사용처 import가 새 경로에 맞게 정리된다
suggestions.reason: The fixture's dashboard page does not import DashboardLayout, making this assertion impossible to meaningfully evaluate. Consider adding a fixture file that actually imports DashboardLayout so this assertion can test a real import path update.

This only checks absence in the outputs directory. If the executor creates new files but forgets to delete the old flat file, the outputs directory wouldn't contain evidence either way. Consider verifying via a git diff or explicit deletion step.

No assertion checks that the sub-components (Sidebar, Header, MobileNav) are separate files rather than inlined in index.tsx. A component could pass all current assertions with everything in a single index.tsx.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions cover structure and conventions well but have a blind spot around the import-update assertion (untestable given the fixture) and don't verify sub-component decomposition into separate files.
